pi_items.source: Golden Gate Bridge is a well-documented, real suspension bridge spanning the Golden Gate strait in San Francisco, California (GPS: 37.8185°N, 122.4738°W). Sygic GPS Navigation app documentation confirms support for POI (Point of Interest) search and routing to landmarks. Source: https://www.latlong.net/place/golden-gate-bridge-a-majestic-landmark-of-san-francisco-33114.html and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Golden_Gate_Bridge

Verifier code at verifier.py line 65 checks: if any(x in activity_name for x in ["Drive", "Navi", "Map", "Route"]). The metadata claims lowercase indicators but the verifier checks for capitalized Android activity names (Drive, Navi, Map, Route). These do not match.
Confirmed in export_result.sh line 8 (RESULT_FILE="/sdcard/task_result.json") and in verifier.py line 41 (copy_from_env("/sdcard/task_result.json", temp_json.name))
Confirmed in export_result.sh line 9 (SCREENSHOT_FILE="/sdcard/task_final.png") and line 12 (screencap -p "$SCREENSHOT_FILE")

privileged_info_summary: The task targets the Golden Gate Bridge, a real suspension bridge in San Francisco (coordinates 37.8185°N, 122.4738°W), searchable and navigable via the Sygic GPS Navigation app. The task uses a non-synthetic environment based on real Sygic GPS software and real map/routing data. Task results are exported to /sdcard/task_result.json and /sdcard/task_final.png. Notably, the expected_activity_indicators metadata field lists lowercase values ["navi", "drive", "simulation", "demo"] but the verifier checks for capitalized Android activity names ["Drive", "Navi", "Map", "Route"], which do not match exactly.
